On May 23rd, 2023, the Alachua County Sheriff's office, located in Florida, responded to a 911 call from a distressed man stating that his roommate had stabbed him in the neck and he was pouring blood. Dispatch quickly put a call out to all units in the area, and law enforcement sped to the scene because the terrified victim was unable to give any additional details during the call, as he feared the suspect was still in the home. Deputies move in, quickly and carefully to clear the house. As they canvas the space, room by room, they find mostly what one may expect from the unkempt bachelor pad of a few single middle-aged men, with one exception, a lot of blood. The majority of it is found in the area surrounding a computer desk in one of the bedrooms, as well as on the bathroom floor, presumably where the victim obtained the bloodied rag he was seen holding against his neck to stem the flow of blood. Ultimately, no additional individuals are located inside the home, and the crime scene is locked down to preserve evidence.

Bloody articles need to be dried as much as possible and then packaged in several layers or containers of paper. The paper bag itself is more appropriate than plastic, since it promotes drying. Interestingly enough, samples remaining wet actually degrade faster, though after drying, they should be frozen. It's important not to place a wet biological sample into a package that is too small, because that delays drying and increases the chances of spoiling any evidentiary value. Given the immediate circumstances, placing evidence in a paper bag is likely the best the officers can do until more advanced evidence collection technicians arrive on scene.

Nicked, as it turned out, would be putting it lightly. The victim, who identified himself as Jeremy Newman, was tended to by the first deputy to arrive at the scene after Jeremy exited the home. That deputy described the encounter in his incident report. Jeremy was bleeding heavily, his shirt saturated in blood along with his hands, arms, body, and his long hair. According to Jeremy, he'd been working at his home desk when he noticed Omar walk up behind him. Not thinking anything of it, he'd continued working. However, moments later, he felt a sharp pain in his neck, and reaching back, he felt the knife that had been plunged into him, slicing his hand on the blade as he removed it. When he'd asked Omar why he'd attacked him, Omar allegedly replied, it was instinctual, taking the knife back and walking out of the room. Jeremy was transported to Shans Emergency Room, where he continued to give his shocking tale. He explained that he was an Australian native who had moved to Florida in 2014 to continue his post-doctoral research at UF. Omar, it seems, had moved in with him and a third roommate about nine months prior, though the pair had been friends for years. Things had gone smoothly up until a few weeks before, when Omar accused Jeremy of bothering his cat, which Jeremy vehemently denied. However, it's alleged that Omar's behavior became progressively more odd. He allegedly told Jeremy at one point, I'm not above killing you. Thankfully, medical evaluations determined Jeremy's wounds to be non-fatal, though additional details are not known.

Background checks revealed that Omar had no criminal history and no known history of violence. However, Jeremy did tell law enforcement that he felt Omar suffered from paranoid schizophrenia, though it's important to note that these claims are unsubstantiated. What we do know is that Omar was charged with one count of attempted murder and booked at Alachua County Jail on a $1 million bond. Court records, however, show that on July 27th, 2023, Omar was found to be incompetent to stand trial, following a psychological evaluation, and was committed to the Department of Children and Families.
